item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
You actually made me feel really sad about the feelings of fruits and vegetables, you absolute monster.

Upvoted

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Then I have done my job! :D Thanks!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Never felt more strongly about the wellbeing of produce

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well now I have to go back and read it again using GLaDOS' voice for the researchers.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thank you so much! :D

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is absolutely amazing. The Foundation being passive-aggressive to fruit is just beautiful.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is a skip that forces the Foundation to play GLaDOS with itself.

It's also a bunch of fruits.

There is no way I'm not upvoting this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: fuck you fruit +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
My imagination is thinking some really harsh stuff.

Stuff like: "That D-class who called you nice? He's a real screwed up deviant, alright. You know what he likes? He fancies cubes. Like you."

"She didn't have to die. If only you were as round as any other melon, everyone would've just hid in the corners of the hallway. Why did you fit the four-sided hallways so well that you squashed everyone like that… You killed everyone because you're so cubical. You murderous, blood-soaked cube.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Eh, just seems weird-for-the-sake-of-weird. -1.

item.title: Re: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: As author, am cool with this WikiWalk edit :)

item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
Timer Expired. Edits made as discussed below. Author may revert at any time.

The WikiWalk team is proposing edits to this page. All proposed edits will be in a collapsible below this post, and after 7 days if there are no serious objections to the edits, the feedback received will be taken into account as the edits are made. If you have questions not directly related to the article about this project, please pm a member of the WikiWalk Team

+ Show Block
- Hide Block

While SCP-504 doesn't necessarily need the extra attention, both that skip and this one are very enjoyable, humorous skips involving plants what do not plant things, and it is likely the SCPF would look into a potential connection between the two (such as whether SCP-504 is a variant of SCP-2941). As such, I am recommending the following edits:

Changing this:

Addendum A: List of currently-contained SCP-2941 instances

To this:

Addendum A: List of currently-contained SCP-2941 instances.1

If anyone has other/better examples that could be used here please leave a comment saying so!

Footnotes
1. Investigation into whether SCP-504 is a specialized instance of SCP-2941 is ongoing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is Procedure 110-Montauk.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Damn, and I thought 110-Montauk is cruel! XD

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You fool, you've doomed us all!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: "Who's a good fruit? you are."

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Never before have I laughed so much while feeling so much pity for fruit.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"You are a hideous cube."

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The Foundation: bullying fruit since 19██

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I love this because, unlike most of the -J articles, it could exist in-universe. I can clearly imagine a rookie being told to shout at a melon for a couple of hours as a hazing ritual.

As well as this, the lack of reference to a certain 'aggravating citrus' pleases me. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"You are a hideous cube."

Annnnnnnnnnnnnnnnd upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oh god, poor fruit. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's actually a metaphor about the loss of childhood innocence during one's transition to adulthood.

But seriously, I love this article. It falls into the same area of stupidity that is occupied by SCPs such as 1441 and 1370. Reading through this article is an enjoyable experience because there is enough material to keep me interested in the core concept. Even the addendum is executed well because it's something I can see actually happening.
Definite +1, and I would love to see more articles like this.

item.title: Re: +100!
item.content:encoded: you monster

item.title: Re: +100!
item.content:encoded: (sorry, had to keep you from growing to an absurd size)

item.title: Re: +100!
item.content:encoded: You are a hideous cube.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I stumbled upon a Japanese person who does SCP narration videos, and for one he created these little pixel character versions of some of the 2941 instances, and I had to share because they are adorable:

▼ Show 'em
▲ Hide 'em

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Legitimately one of my favorite SCPs of all time. Adorable fruit, but with the power to destroy everything we know and love. What do we do? Be mean to it. It's absurd and bizarre and I adore it. Have all my upvotes.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This reminds me of that Goosebumps book. It was called something like "It Came from Beneath the Sink." In that Book there was a sponge that if you feared it, it got bigger but, if you where nice to it, it shrunk. SCP-2941 is that in reverse but, not fear meanspirit.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I wish you could take credit for having inspired that. :O

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Hey, I appreciate the kind words. :thumbsup:

Here's the especially boring background that hopefully won't ruin your enjoyment:

▼ Show original terrible idea
▲ Hide original terrible idea

It's all based on the idea that talking to your plants will make them grow better, and there was this whole backstory about a farmer who through *~anomalous means~* (which I never worked out) created these li'l rascals to entertain his daughter but something went horribly wrong along the way, and reports made it to the Foundation about giant watermelons you could see on the horizon, blah blah blah, and I even had a Photoshopped image worked up showing this, but that all got wiped out because it was honestly terrible and focusing on the fruits and veggies and their personal quirks was the most interesting part. So now we have these guys who do need to be talked to, but now to keep them from growing, and it has to be done in a way that's depressing for everybody.

Also, this story came out this summer that kind of stunned me with how close reality mirrors fiction sometimes. I like to think that this was an early SCP-2941-1 breach and the teacher happened to 100% stumble into how to keep things from escalating. I mean, that paragraph underneath the photo/caption? 2941 containment to a freakin' T.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Rediscovered this again today, and it's just delightful. It's how you write a funny skip without it being a -J. A Manual on How to Be Mean to Fruit (And Vegetables). I would love to know what inspired this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Please, Foundation is cold, not cruel

But seriously, I love this article.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Nope.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Hmm… did this have anything to do with the Annoying Orange?
Thanks, you one hundred delightful people; I honestly never expected this silly skip to make it anywhere near that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I love articles that successfully couple a clinical tone an absurd idea. This is great, +1 from me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"You are a hideous cube."
aaaand upvoted

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
No, they are pretty much adorably singly-focused on their individual interests, the little scamps. If the Foundation is doing its job correctly, "confused and sad" would be considered a good day for those cuties.

By the way, I recently came across a leaked photo of SCP-2941-4.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This had me dying at work. Enthusiastic +1 for having the Foundation take its responsibility to be mean to fruit seriously.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Good point. Do the fruits KNOW that being happy results in the deaths of everybody around them or are they just really confused and sad all the time?
Also, what if we ate them?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oh my gosh. I adore this one. I feel bad for the fruits, they probably arent even aware of their dangerous potential to grow huge and just REALLY wanna show you their cool stem-balancing tricks and such.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Attack of the Killer Tomatoes - Containment Breach coverup
please don't bully fruit

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Adored this in the forums, it's even better now, I would give this more than 1 upvote if I could. The last addendum was my favorite part, I have to say.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"You are a hideous cube."

My sides are in orbit. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Violating laws of conservation of mass/energy isn't even worthy of comment among the Foundation staff. Every third thing they contain can do that. Heck, the cafeteria has an anomalous drinks machine!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't know why, but I love the idea of the Foundation devoting effort to being mean to fruits and vegetables. It just tickles me in all my best bits.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This one hit me right in the feels, with the studied tests on what upset the happy fruit people most. I agree that it could do to be a little shorter, but overall it had a big emotional impact on me so I gave it a +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
So…

Is this an SCP about the talking Muppet Vegetables?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You won me over halfway through the test log. I feel like it could due to be a little shorter, because 2/3 of the way in the tests start to feel the same, but it's very funny and the addendum is a great bit to leave off on. I thoroughly enjoyed this. I'm sort of tired of seeing articles that have magic mysterious vocalization powers, but that's a minor quibble. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Liked it in the forums, still as enjoyable on the mainsite.

I LOVE YOU FENNEL LET NO ONE TELL YOU OTHERWISE.

Tests have shown that SCP-2941-2's greatest demoralization occurs when the fabric is simply left covering -2 and no attempt is made to assist while it tries to roll free.

You come across a tortoise in the desert. You flip it over. It struggles to right itself. You watch. You're not helping. Why is that?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Perhaps the Foundation is worried that if they just leave the fruits alone, they'll start finding inner joy through philosophy and introspection or whatever. Instead, they need to encourage trying to do what makes them happy, but never allow it to actually succeed. After all, isn't it more demoralizing to try and fail than to never even be able to try?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Demoralizing fruit? I can get behind it. This needs some work on tone, however, and the final addendum is a little lolFoundation.

Also, for the ones that like moving, the banana especially, why not just strap them down so they can't? Some of the procedures seem a little involved for what's being accomplished.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Can't tell if this was on purpose or a coincidence.

While coming up with names, I thought of Clarissa's brother for some reason, and well, he's terrible!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I was already going to upvote based on the rest of the article. Well-written, interesting concept yet slightly humourous. Then I got to the punchline. Enjoy all of my +1, good author.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Names producing the most severe demoralization vectors so far include "Ferguson"

Can't tell if this was on purpose or a coincidence.

Still, the concept and the way it was handled was enough to make me take pause while reading through the table. In this case, an article doesn't need a backstory, because it's plain to see that these are happy fruits that need to stay sad, and I enjoyed it overall.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I personally thought this was brilliant, and it honestly made me laugh out loud a couple times, particularly at the lines Foundation personnel have to say to the 2941 instances ("you are a hideous cube"). Yet, even though it's humorous, it doesn't strike me as something that should be a -J, since there are actual dangers involved with letting the instances get too big. The incident report at the end was gold. I commend you, author, and hope I can do even half as well with my first SCP.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thanks for the kind words, as well as the in-depth crit. I really do appreciate it.
I'm swamped at work right now, but later tonight I will try to address your notes.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I still don't really understand this one. It is fruit that grows when you are nice to it and shrinks when you are mean?

Does this violate conservation?

How does the fennel survive 16 days of darkness?

Is there something deeper here than "weird plants" that I am missing?
